pi_items.source: Water supply LCA standard practice: 1 m³ = 1000 liters is the conventional functional unit for drinking water LCA studies (confirmed via Environmental Science & Technology research)

These keywords correspond to real LCIA impact categories: 'energy' (CED measured in MJ), 'warming' (Global Warming Potential in TRACI 2.1), 'fossil' and 'fuel' (Fossil Fuel Depletion category), 'resource' (Resource Depletion category)

MJ is the standard unit for Cumulative Energy Demand calculations (verified via ecoinvent and ask.openLCA documentation). '1000' and 'liter' represent the functional unit. 'energy' is the impact category type.
USLCI database Spring 2025 update confirms inclusion of 23 drinking water acquisition, treatment and distribution processes (verified via LCA Commons and GitHub USLCI-content repository)
Task specification and environment setup scripts confirm /home/ga/LCA_Results/ is created by setup_task.sh for CSV output storage
Task specification and environment setup scripts confirm /home/ga/LCA_Results/ is created by setup_task.sh for summary text output storage
USLCI database documentation and LCA Commons website confirm USLCI database contains more than 660 processes as of 2025

pi_items.verified_value: 23 drinking water acquisition, treatment and distribution processes
pi_items.source: USLCI Spring 2025 Quarterly Release (LCA Commons website) documents 23 drinking water processes developed by EPA and ERG

pi_items.metadata_value: TRACI 2.1 and Cumulative Energy Demand methods assumed available
pi_items.verified_value: Both TRACI 2.1 and Cumulative Energy Demand (CED) are real, published EPA/ecoinvent methods available in openLCA
pi_items.source: openLCA LCIA method package includes TRACI 2.1 (EPA) and CED methods; both confirmed available for water impact assessment

privileged_info_summary: The water_energy_demand task uses real data from the USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory) database, which contains 660+ processes including 23 drinking water acquisition, treatment, and distribution processes. The task requires calculating energy demand for 1,000 liters of tap water (1 m³), which is the standard functional unit for water supply LCA studies. Results are analyzed using real LCIA methods (TRACI 2.1 or Cumulative Energy Demand) that measure impacts in MJ units. CSV results are expected to contain keywords related to energy impacts (energy, fossil fuel, resource depletion, global warming), and the summary text must report the Process Name, 1,000-liter functional unit, and Total Energy Demand in MJ. All metadata claims regarding functional units, keywords, USLCI database content, and applicable LCIA methods have been independently verified against authoritative sources including EPA documentation, research publications, and LCA Commons database release notes.
